name: Foothills
description_short: Foothills Federal Credit Union is a non-profit financial cooperative serving Loudon, Monroe, and Blount Counties in Tennessee with member-owned banking and lending services.
description_long:
Foothills Federal Credit Union was originally chartered in 1962 as UC Employees Credit Union to serve Union Carbide Company employees in Loudon, Tennessee. The organization expanded its charter in 1983 to include multiple employee groups, rebranded as Foothills Federal Credit Union in 2001, and received a three-county community charter in 2005 covering Loudon, Monroe, and Blount Counties. Today it operates as a member-owned, non-profit financial institution governed by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA).

Foothills FCU offers a comprehensive range of savings and lending products. On the savings side, they provide Smart Savings Accounts (minimum $5.00), Christmas & Vacation Club Accounts, Smart Budget Accounts, Certificates of Deposit (6-month to 3-year terms with $1,000 minimum), and IRA accounts including Traditional, Roth, Coverdell ESA, and SEP options. For borrowing, they offer auto loans, share-secured loans, and personal loans. All member deposits are federally insured to $250,000 by the National Credit Union Share Insurance Fund.

As a credit union rather than a bank, Foothills distinguishes itself through its non-profit structure and member-ownership model. The organization is run by volunteer members elected to the Board of Directors and Supervisory Committee, with all members invited to participate in annual meetings. They emphasize lower fees (not priced to generate revenue), generally lower loan rates compared to banks, and a mission focused on member interests rather than shareholder profits. Their routing number is 264280022.

The primary limitation is geographic eligibility—membership is restricted to individuals who live, work, or worship in the three designated Tennessee counties. While they offer standard credit union services, the website does not detail current interest rates, specific loan terms, or online banking features beyond mentioning online banking availability. For consumers in the Foothills service area, this represents a legitimate local alternative to traditional banking, but out-of-area residents cannot join.
services: Smart Savings Accounts with daily dividend accrual and quarterly credit
Christmas & Vacation Club Accounts with payroll deduction deposit options
Smart Budget Account for vacation or tax savings with no minimum balance
Certificates of Deposit in 6-month, 1-year, 2-year, and 3-year terms
Traditional IRA Accounts with daily dividends
Online Banking access with account management
pros: Non-profit structure with volunteer member-led governance focused on member interests rather than shareholder profits
Federal deposit insurance up to $250,000 per account through NCUSIF
Flexible savings options including Christmas Club, Budget Account, and multiple IRA types with low or no minimums
Member-oriented mission with annual meetings open to all members for participation and oversight
Membership in Tennessee Credit Union League and Knoxville Area Chapter of Credit Unions for additional resources
Certificates of Deposit available in multiple terms (6 months to 3 years) with stated higher-than-average dividends
Community focus with three-county service area and active member engagement (Shred Day events, text alerts)
cons: Geographic membership restriction—limited to residents, employees, and worshippers in Loudon, Monroe, or Blount Counties only
Website lacks specific current interest rates, dividend rates, and detailed loan term information needed for rate shopping
No information provided about online banking capabilities, mobile app features, ATM network access, or digital service depth
Limited details on lending products—auto and personal loans mentioned but without rates, terms, or approval requirements
No information about customer service hours, branch locations beyond Loudon reference, or phone banking availability
best_for: Tennessee residents in Loudon, Monroe, or Blount Counties seeking community-based banking with lower fees
Local employees and members of eligible groups wanting member-owned financial institution benefits
Savers interested in CD laddering or IRA accounts with federally insured deposits and tax advantages
Borrowers seeking lower-rate personal or auto loans through a non-profit credit union structure
diagnosis: Foothills Federal Credit Union is best suited for individuals living or working in Tennessee's Loudon, Monroe, or Blount Counties who value non-profit, member-owned banking with lower fees and community governance. The main caveat is strict geographic eligibility—out-of-area consumers cannot join, and the website provides insufficient rate and product detail for proper comparison shopping.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Foothills — Credit Union in LOUDON, TN | CreditDoc
meta_description: Foothills — NCUA-insured credit union in LOUDON, TN. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
